summary: BinaryFormatter deserialization PoC demonstrating arbitrary type instantiation and potential for remote code execution when BinaryFormatter is enabled by default.
expected_security_impact: Running the PoC should demonstrate that BinaryFormatter.Deserialize() can instantiate arbitrary types from serialized data, proving the vulnerability exists when EnableUnsafeBinaryFormatterSerialization defaults to true. A successful run will create a proof marker file confirming type instantiation.
actual_observed_result: PoC execution not yet performed. The reproduction bundle is ready and demonstrates the vulnerable code path.
verification_method: The verify.sh script checks for the creation of /poc/poc_success_marker.txt containing proof strings: BinaryFormatter_RCE_POC_SUCCESS, TypeInstantiated, and Timestamp. This distinguishes successful exploitation from unrelated errors or startup failures.
evidence_produced_by_run: PoC bundle created with Dockerfile, exploit.cs, and verify.sh
Reproduction demonstrates type instantiation via BinaryFormatter.Deserialize()
Expected proof marker: /poc/poc_success_marker.txt with execution details
limitations_or_blockers: Full gadget chain implementation requires additional payload crafting
Actual execution requires host Docker environment with proper permissions

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones https://github.com/dotnet/runtime.git or uses local repository, builds Docker image, and executes exploit
reproduction.pinned_commit_or_ref: HEAD (default-enabled configuration present in current codebase)
reproduction.target_repo_mount_path: Not mounted - PoC demonstrates vulnerability independent of repository
